summary: Bitcoin dominance (BTC.D = BTC market cap / total crypto market cap) is the master regime gauge for capital rotation *within* crypto. When dominance rises, capital concentrates in Bitcoin — typically early-bull accumulation, ETF-driven legs, or risk-off flight-to-quality. When dominance falls, capital rotates down the risk curve into altcoins ('alt-season'), historically a late-cycle, risk-on, euphoria signal. The Altcoin Season Index (share of top-50 alts beating BTC over 90 days) operationalizes it: >75 = alt-season, <25 = Bitcoin season. A backtester should treat dominance as a regime filter, not a directional bet on total market cap.

archetypes.name: BTC dominance regime (risk-on/off rotation within crypto)
archetypes.mechanism: Crypto capital rotates along a risk curve anchored by Bitcoin. In early-bull and risk-off phases, money concentrates in BTC (the 'reserve asset' of crypto) and dominance rises; the 2024-2025 spot-ETF era amplified this because ETF flows are overwhelmingly BTC-only, structurally lifting dominance. In late-bull euphoria, having made multiples on BTC, capital chases higher-beta alts and dominance falls. The people 'on the other side' of a dominance call are traders fighting the prevailing regime — buying alts in a BTC-dominant tape (bleed vs BTC) or hiding in BTC during a genuine alt-season (underperformance). The signal is the *regime*, not the level of any single coin.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC.D swings of 15-35 percentage points across a full cycle; alt baskets can out/under-perform BTC by 50-200%+ over a rotation
archetypes.recurrence: every cycle: BTC-led accumulation -> mid-cycle ETH/large-cap rotation -> late-cycle alt/meme blow-off -> risk-off flight back to BTC
leading_signals: Altcoin Season Index (>75 alt-season / <25 BTC season)
ETH/BTC ratio and its 50/200-day MA crosses
TOTAL2 / TOTAL ratio (alt share of market cap)
BTC spot-ETF net flows vs alt-ETF/alt-fund flows

historical_instances.trigger: 2021 alt-season: BTC dominance collapses as DOGE/BNB/altcoins blow off.
historical_instances.description: Through early 2021 BTC dominance fell from ~73% (January) to ~40% (mid-May) as capital rotated into BNB, DOGE, and the broad altcoin complex — a textbook late-cycle alt-season. The dominance bottom and alt euphoria immediately preceded the May 19 2021 leverage-driven crash.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Alt volumes overtook BTC; alt-season euphoria peaked just before the May-19 crash.

historical_instances.trigger: ETF-era BTC-led leg: BTC sets records ($122,838 on Jul 14 2025; $126,210 ATH Oct 6 2025) while dominance stays elevated.
historical_instances.description: The 2024-2025 cycle was unusually BTC-dominant: spot-ETF flows (BTC-only at first) concentrated demand in Bitcoin, which printed $122,838 on 2025-07-14 and an all-time high of $126,210 on 2025-10-06 while altcoins broadly lagged — the opposite of a classic alt-season. Dominance held in the high-50s/low-60s%, frustrating alt-season callers.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Spot-BTC-ETF inflows concentrated demand in BTC; alt share (TOTAL2) suppressed.

historical_instances.trigger: Bitcoin Season persists: BTC.D ~58-58.7%, Altcoin Season Index ~49, ~249 days since the last alt-season.
historical_instances.description: As of early June 2026 BTC dominance is ~58-58.7% with the Altcoin Season Index ~49 (below the 75 alt-season threshold) and ~249 days since the last confirmed alt-season — firmly 'Bitcoin Season.' A widely-circulated claim of a 'June 2026 alt-season rally' (with BTC at a $122,800 ATH and a $4.13T total market cap) is INCORRECT: those figures are a stale 2025 snapshot. In reality BTC was ~$63,500 and falling (-18% over the prior week) and total market cap was ~$2.35-2.5T — a risk-off drawdown, not an alt-season.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Risk-off drawdown; no alt-season rotation despite a minor 59.9%->58.7% dominance dip.

archetypes.name: Alt-season extreme as a late-cycle top signal
archetypes.mechanism: An alt-season is the last leg of the crypto risk curve: once BTC and ETH have run, the most speculative capital chases low-quality alts and memecoins. Historically the dominance *bottom* (alts most euphoric) coincides with cycle tops, because it marks maximum risk appetite and leverage with no higher-beta buyer left. The reversal — a sharp dominance spike as alts crater faster than BTC — is the risk-off 'flight to (crypto) quality' that confirms the regime has flipped. Traders fading the euphoria win; late alt-chasers are left holding the highest-beta bag into the drawdown.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: post-peak alt baskets routinely draw down 70-90%+; BTC.D rebounds 15-30pp off the bottom
archetypes.typical_lag: the top signal is coincident-to-leading by days/weeks; confirmation on the dominance reversal
archetypes.typical_duration: the unwind runs the length of the ensuing bear (months-to-a-year)
archetypes.recurrence: once per cycle at the euphoria peak
leading_signals: ETH/BTC and alt/BTC ratios parabolic then rolling over

historical_instances.trigger: ICO-era alt-mania drives BTC dominance to a cycle low (~33%) at the top.
historical_instances.description: At the January 2018 peak, ICO/altcoin mania drove BTC dominance down to ~33% as speculative capital flooded alts — the dominance bottom marked the cycle top, and the subsequent bear wiped 80-95% off most alts.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Alt volume share peaked; the 2018 bear followed.

historical_instances.trigger: 2021 alt-season bottom in dominance precedes the May-19 leverage crash.
historical_instances.description: The May 2021 dominance low (~40%) and peak alt euphoria immediately preceded the May-19 2021 leverage-driven crash (BTC -30%+ intraday). Classic alt-season-exhaustion -> risk-off reversal, with dominance rebounding as alts fell faster than BTC.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Flight to BTC quality as leverage unwound; see [[technical-signals]] and [[memecoin-mania]].
